content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 29, 1988


Stephen C. Gaither
Gaither & Company
The Adobe Creek Lodge Initiative
26200 Moody Road
Los Altos Hills, CA  94022

Re:  Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. I-88-131

Dear Mr. Gaither:

     You have asked for advice concerning various actions taken
by the Los Altos Hills City Council.

     The Commission's Legal Division does provide written advice
to persons with questions about their duties under the
Political Reform Act.1/ (Section 83114(b).) We do not provide
advice to third parties about another person's duties unless
the third party has been authorized to request advice by the
person whose duties are in question.  (Regulation
18329(b)(8)(B).)  Therefore, we cannot provide advice on the
question you have raised.  However, I have referred your letter
to our Enforcement Division for review.  If you have any
questions, that division can be reached at (916) 322-6441.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]

                                        Diane M. Griffiths
                                        General Counsel

DMG:plh
cc:  Roger Brown

_________________________
1/  Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
indicated.  Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code
of Regulations Section 18000, et seq.  All references to
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code
of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

THE ADOBE CREEK LODGE INITIATIVE


March 24, 1988


Mr. Bruce Robeck
Political Reform Consultant
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
428 J Street, Suite 800
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Re:  Los Altos Hills General Municipal Election

Dear Robeck:

Based on the enclosed materials, I am respectfully requesting your opinion as
to whether the Town of Los Altos Hills comes within the reporting requirements
of your agency.

As the enclosed flyer establishes, the Town of Los Altos Hills produced and
distributed political campaign materials.  The enclosed copy of my letter to
the District Attorney explains how the flyer constitutes political persuasion
rather than factual statements.  Further explanation is provided in the
enclosed documentation.

The Town of Los Altos Hills will also spend money to host and publicize a
"Special Meeting" of the Town of Los Altos Hills City Council to present
information on Ballot Measure B.  I believe that this will represent a
political campaign expenditure because at the same time that the Town will
present what it considers to be "factual information" to the public, it will
also be presenting "political arguments" and opinions/misrepresentations (see
enclosed analysis by Attorney Etzel).

The Town of Los Altos Hills is also engaging in campaign expenditures in that
the Mayor has repeatedly directed the City Attorney and Staff to use their
time to find ways to defeat Measure B and challenge me individually as a
candidate for City Council.

Please contact me for any further information you require.  Thank you in
advance for your prompt attention to this matter.

Respectfully yours,


Stephen C. Gaither

Enclosures


Gaither & Company • 26200 Moody Road • Los Altos Hills, CA 94022 • (415) 949-2620
